- [**k-Nearest Neighbours Regression**](tutorials/machine-learning/knn-regression.qmd) -- Local averaging for non-parametric regression

## [Clinical Biostatistics](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/index.qmd)

- [**Adaptive Trial Designs**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/adaptive-design.qmd) -- Pre-specified modifications to trial conduct based on interim data
- [**Alpha-Spending Functions**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/alpha-spending.qmd) -- Flexible interim alpha allocation via Lan-DeMets spending functions
- [**Baseline Adjustment with ANCOVA**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/baseline-adjustment-ancova.qmd) -- Adjusting for baseline covariates to improve precision and address regression to the mean
- [**Bland-Altman Limits of Agreement**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/bland-altman-analysis.qmd) -- Graphical comparison of two measurement methods on the same subjects
- [**Blinding Procedures**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/blinding-procedures.qmd) -- Masking participants, investigators, assessors, and analysts
- [**Block Randomisation**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/block-randomization.qmd) -- Random permutations within fixed blocks for balanced allocation
- [**Clinical Equivalence Trials**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/equivalence-clinical.qmd) -- Two one-sided tests (TOST) for bioequivalence and clinical equivalence
- [**Cluster-Randomised Trials**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/cluster-rct.qmd) -- Randomisation of clusters (clinics, schools) rather than individuals
- [**Cohen's Kappa**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/cohens-kappa.qmd) -- Chance-corrected agreement between two raters on categorical data
- [**Conditional Power**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/conditional-power.qmd) -- Probability of eventual trial success given interim data
- [**Crossover RCT Design**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/rct-design-crossover.qmd) -- Within-subject comparison across treatment periods with washout
- [**Cutpoint Selection**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/cutpoint-selection.qmd) -- Youden's J, cost-based, and closest-to-(0,1) criteria for diagnostic thresholds
- [**Diagnostic Test Accuracy**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/diagnostic-accuracy.qmd) -- Sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, likelihood ratios, and ROC analysis for binary diagnostic tests
- [**Factorial Trials**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/factorial-trial.qmd) -- Simultaneously evaluating multiple interventions via a factorial design
- [**ITT vs Per-Protocol Analysis**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/itt-vs-pp-analysis.qmd) -- Primary analyses under the intent-to-treat and per-protocol principles
- [**Interim Analyses and Group Sequential Designs**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/interim-analysis-group-sequential.qmd) -- Pre-planned interim looks with alpha-spending and early-stopping boundaries
- [**Intraclass Correlation Coefficient (ICC)**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/icc-continuous-agreement.qmd) -- Absolute agreement and consistency for continuous inter-rater reliability
- [**Likelihood Ratios**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/likelihood-ratios.qmd) -- LR+ and LR- as prevalence-independent summaries of diagnostic performance
- [**Minimisation Algorithm**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/minimization-algorithm.qmd) -- Covariate-adaptive allocation that prospectively minimises imbalance
- [**Missing Data in RCTs**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/missing-data-rct.qmd) -- MCAR, MAR, MNAR and their implications for primary analysis
- [**Multiple Imputation**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/multiple-imputation.qmd) -- Imputing multiple plausible values and combining via Rubin's rules
- [**Non-Inferiority Margin Selection**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/non-inferiority-margin.qmd) -- Defining the clinically acceptable maximum inferiority
- [**O'Brien-Fleming Boundary**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/obrien-fleming-boundary.qmd) -- Conservative early stopping boundary in group-sequential trials
- [**Parallel-Group RCT Design**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/rct-design-parallel.qmd) -- The two-arm randomised controlled trial: structure, analysis, and reporting
- [**Pocock Boundary**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/pocock-boundary.qmd) -- Constant nominal alpha across interim analyses
- [**Predictive Values and Prevalence**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/predictive-values-prevalence.qmd) -- How disease prevalence determines PPV and NPV via Bayes' theorem
- [**ROC Analysis**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/roc-analysis.qmd) -- Receiver Operating Characteristic curves and area under the curve
- [**Randomisation Methods**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/randomization-methods.qmd) -- Simple, block, and stratified randomisation for RCT allocation
- [**Reliability and Cronbach's Alpha**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/reliability-cronbach-alpha.qmd) -- Internal consistency of multi-item scales
- [**Sample Size Re-Estimation**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/sample-size-reestimation.qmd) -- Updating trial sample size mid-study using blinded or unblinded nuisance parameter estimates
- [**Sensitivity Analyses in Clinical Trials**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/sensitivity-analysis-clinical.qmd) -- Exploring robustness of conclusions to assumptions about missing data and model choice
- [**Stepped-Wedge Trial**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/stepped-wedge-trial.qmd) -- Sequential rollout cluster design with all clusters eventually treated
- [**Stratified Randomisation**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/stratified-randomization.qmd) -- Separate randomisation lists within levels of baseline covariates
- [**Subgroup Analyses**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/subgroup-analysis.qmd) -- Pre-specified subgroup effects and treatment-by-subgroup interaction tests
- [**Subgroup Forest Plots**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/forest-plot-subgroup.qmd) -- Visual summary of subgroup-specific effects and interaction tests
- [**Weighted Kappa**](tutorials/clinical-biostatistics/weighted-kappa.qmd) -- Ordinal inter-rater agreement with linear or quadratic weights

## [Meta-Analysis](tutorials/meta-analysis/index.qmd)

- [**Bivariate SROC Curves**](tutorials/meta-analysis/bivariate-sroc.qmd) -- Hierarchical summary ROC modelling for diagnostic-accuracy meta-analysis
- [**Converting Between Effect Sizes**](tutorials/meta-analysis/effect-size-conversion.qmd) -- Moving between standardised mean differences, correlations, and odds ratios
- [**Cumulative Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/cumulative-meta-analysis.qmd) -- Sequentially pooling studies in the order they were published
- [**Egger's Test for Funnel Asymmetry**](tutorials/meta-analysis/egger-test.qmd) -- Formal regression test for small-study effects and publication bias
- [**Fixed-Effect vs. Random-Effects Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/fixed-vs-random-effects.qmd) -- Two ways to pool study-level effect sizes, and the consequences of each for inference and interpretation
- [**Forest Plots in Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/forest-plot-meta.qmd) -- Standard visualisation of per-study effects, weights, and the pooled estimate
- [**Funnel Plots**](tutorials/meta-analysis/funnel-plot-meta.qmd) -- Visual inspection for small-study effects and publication bias
- [**Individual Participant Data Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/ipd-meta-analysis.qmd) -- Two-stage vs one-stage analysis of raw data pooled across studies
- [**Leave-One-Out Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/leave-one-out.qmd) -- Sensitivity of the pooled estimate to each individual study
- [**Meta-Analysis of Diagnostic Accuracy**](tutorials/meta-analysis/diagnostic-test-meta.qmd) -- Bivariate and HSROC models for pooling diagnostic test performance
- [**Meta-Regression**](tutorials/meta-analysis/meta-regression.qmd) -- Explaining between-study heterogeneity with study-level covariates
- [**NMA Consistency and Inconsistency**](tutorials/meta-analysis/nma-consistency.qmd) -- Loop-specific and global tests for direct-indirect evidence agreement
- [**NMA League Tables**](tutorials/meta-analysis/nma-league-tables.qmd) -- Pairwise treatment contrasts in a compact triangular grid
- [**Network Meta-Analysis: Introduction**](tutorials/meta-analysis/network-meta-analysis-intro.qmd) -- Combining direct and indirect comparisons across multiple treatments
- [**Pooling Correlation Coefficients**](tutorials/meta-analysis/correlation-pooling.qmd) -- Fisher z transformation for meta-analysis of correlations
- [**Pooling Log-Odds Ratios**](tutorials/meta-analysis/log-odds-ratio-pooling.qmd) -- Fixed and random-effects meta-analysis of binary outcomes on the log scale
- [**Pooling Risk Ratios**](tutorials/meta-analysis/risk-ratio-pooling.qmd) -- Meta-analysis of binary outcomes on the risk-ratio scale
- [**Prediction Intervals in Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/prediction-interval-meta.qmd) -- The plausible range of a future study's true effect under random-effects
- [**Quantifying Heterogeneity: Q and I^2**](tutorials/meta-analysis/heterogeneity-q-i2.qmd) -- Cochran's Q, I-squared, and their interpretation in meta-analysis
- [**SUCRA Rankings**](tutorials/meta-analysis/nma-sucra.qmd) -- Surface Under the Cumulative Ranking curve for treatment comparisons
- [**Selection Models for Publication Bias**](tutorials/meta-analysis/selection-models-bias.qmd) -- Weighted-distribution models for adjusting meta-analysis for selection
- [**Standardised Mean Difference: Hedges' g**](tutorials/meta-analysis/smd-hedges-g.qmd) -- Small-sample-corrected standardised effect size for continuous outcomes
- [**Subgroup Meta-Analysis**](tutorials/meta-analysis/subgroup-meta-analysis.qmd) -- Pooling within strata and testing for between-subgroup heterogeneity
- [**Tau-Squared Estimation**](tutorials/meta-analysis/tau-squared-estimation.qmd) -- Estimators of between-study variance: DL, REML, PM, SJ, EB
- [**Trim-and-Fill**](tutorials/meta-analysis/trim-and-fill.qmd) -- Adjusting the pooled estimate for apparent funnel asymmetry

## [Experimental Design](tutorials/experimental-design/index.qmd)

- [**2^k Factorial Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/factorial-2k.qmd) -- Full factorial experiments with k two-level factors
- [**3^k Factorial Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/factorial-3k.qmd) -- Full factorial experiments with k three-level factors
- [**Balanced Incomplete Block Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/balanced-incomplete-block.qmd) -- BIBDs when block size is smaller than treatment count
- [**Box-Behnken Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/box-behnken-design.qmd) -- Three-level rotatable designs for RSM without factorial-corner runs
- [**Central Composite Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/central-composite-design.qmd) -- Response-surface designs combining factorial, axial, and centre points
- [**Completely Randomised Design**](tutorials/experimental-design/completely-randomized-design.qmd) -- The simplest experimental design: random assignment of treatments to units
- [**Constrained Mixture Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/constrained-mixture.qmd) -- Mixture experiments with lower and upper bounds on components
- [**Crossover Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/crossover-designs.qmd) -- Within-subject comparison of treatments with washout and carryover consideration
- [**Desirability Functions**](tutorials/experimental-design/desirability-function.qmd) -- Multi-response optimisation via geometric-mean desirability scores
- [**Fractional Factorial Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/fractional-factorial-design.qmd) -- A fraction of a 2^k design: economical screening with acceptable confounding
- [**Graeco-Latin Square Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/graeco-latin-square.qmd) -- Blocking on three nuisance factors by superimposing two orthogonal Latin squares
- [**Latin Square Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/latin-square.qmd) -- Blocking on two nuisance factors via a Latin-square arrangement
- [**Method of Steepest Ascent**](tutorials/experimental-design/steepest-ascent.qmd) -- Moving along the gradient of a first-order response surface toward the optimum
- [**Optimal Designs: D, A, I**](tutorials/experimental-design/optimal-design-d-a-i.qmd) -- D-, A-, I-, and G-optimality criteria for computer-generated designs
- [**Orthogonal Arrays**](tutorials/experimental-design/orthogonal-arrays.qmd) -- Tabulated fractional-factorial arrays L4, L8, L9, L16, L27
- [**Plackett-Burman Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/plackett-burman.qmd) -- Highly efficient two-level screening designs for many factors
- [**Power Analysis for DOE**](tutorials/experimental-design/power-in-doe.qmd) -- Calculating power for detecting main effects and interactions in designed experiments
- [**Principles of Blocking**](tutorials/experimental-design/blocking-principles.qmd) -- Local control of nuisance variability for precision gains
- [**Randomisation in Design**](tutorials/experimental-design/randomization-in-design.qmd) -- Purpose, procedures, and role of randomisation in experimental design
- [**Randomised Complete Block Design**](tutorials/experimental-design/randomised-complete-block.qmd) -- Blocking to control known nuisance variation, with construction, analysis, and interpretation in R
- [**Repeated-Measures Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/repeated-measures-design.qmd) -- Designs with a within-subject factor: multiple measurements per unit
- [**Resolution and Aliasing**](tutorials/experimental-design/resolution-aliasing.qmd) -- Understanding the confounding structure of fractional factorial designs
- [**Response Surface Methodology**](tutorials/experimental-design/response-surface-methodology.qmd) -- Optimising processes via second-order polynomial response surfaces
- [**Robust Parameter Design**](tutorials/experimental-design/robust-parameter-design.qmd) -- Choosing controllable factor levels to minimise response variance across noise factors
- [**Signal-to-Noise Ratios**](tutorials/experimental-design/signal-to-noise-ratio.qmd) -- Taguchi's SNR metrics combining mean and variance
- [**Simplex Centroid Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/mixture-centroid.qmd) -- Mixture designs including centroids of all sub-simplices
- [**Simplex Lattice Mixture Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/mixture-designs-simplex.qmd) -- Experimental designs for component-proportion factors that sum to 1
- [**Split-Plot Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/split-plot-design.qmd) -- Designs with hard-to-change and easy-to-change factors
- [**Strip-Plot Designs**](tutorials/experimental-design/strip-plot-design.qmd) -- Designs with two hard-to-change factors applied to perpendicular strips
- [**Taguchi Methods**](tutorials/experimental-design/taguchi-methods.qmd) -- Orthogonal-array designs and signal-to-noise optimisation for quality engineering
